dayList.section: Half day city tour of Buenos Aires

decodedJson.description:
Today you will discover the wonders of the cosmopolitan city of Buenos Aires, capital of Argentina. We will visit the city square of Plaza de Mayo, also known as the main foundation site of Buenos Aires. Now it is a museum with centuries of history within. Then we will stop at historic neighborhoods such as La Boca and Recoleta. Continuing on, Puerto Madero awaits with its dikes and distinguished gastronomy, as well as the imposing Puente de la Mujer that crosses the Rio de la Plata, creating a silhouette. Following, we will visit the Floralis. The six steel petals open at 8am each morning, and close at midnight. Then we will go to the Recoleta Cemetery, one of the most beautiful cemeteries in the world. Finally, we will visit the widest avenue in all of South America, the “9 de Julio Avenue”.  

 

Itinerary:
Plaza de Mayo（Outside Visit Cathedral、Casa Rosada）→ La Boca & Recoleta → Puerto Madero → Floralis → Recoleta Cemetery (include ticket) → 9 de Julio Avenue

 

Dinner: Dinner Tango Show

content.sceniceDesc: The Plaza de Mayo is the oldest public square in Buenos Aires and has been the scene of many of the most important events in the city's history. The square is named after the Argentine revolution, which began on May 25, 1810. Around the square are several important buildings: the Cabildo, the Metropolitan Cathedral, where Pope Francis conducted mass for 20 years, the Casa Rosada, seat of the national government, the national revenue office (AFIP), the national bank, and the intelligence secretariat.

Named "Floralis Genérica" in homage to all flowers, this 20m-high, 18-tonne aluminum and stainless-steel sculpture dominates the Plaza de las Naciones Unidas. Unveiled in the year 2002, it was probably the world's first mobile public sculpture to be controlled by hydraulics and photoelectric sensors. The six steel petals open at 8am each morning, and close at midnight.

The Recoleta Cemetery is an eerily beautiful place, with shadowed walkways and towering marble mausoleums rich in Art Deco, Art Nouveau, baroque and neo-gothic architectural styles, with Masonic symbols and powerful religious iconographies. Over 90 of its tombs are listed as national historical monuments. The most visited tombs are those of Eva Perón and former Argentine presidents Sarmiento and Raúl Alfonsín.

decodedJson.description: Today we will visit an estancia (farm) located in the pampas so as to enjoy a perfect day in the countryside and to learn about the typical character of Argentina, the Gaucho. There, it is possible to ride a horse or to take a trip in one of the carriages available in the Estancia either before or after trying the typical Asado (barbecue) of Argentina together with some empanadas (meat pastries) and accompanied by some local wine and gaucho music and dances.

decodedJson.description:
Today you can freely travel in Buenos Aires or choose to participate in activities at your own expense. The optional tour is considered from 2 travelers, please take note all optionals are subject to availability.

 

Option 1: Half Day Buenos Aires Tigre y Delta

Lower Delta, where its stunning beauty is tinged with ocher and green. Flowers captivate us with its aroma and with the particular color of the water we make a walk through our senses. In the course, we can observe, channels outlined by nature, traditional rowing clubs, the Deliberative Council, the Tigre Club (formerly "Tiger Hotel"), the Sarmiento Museum, the life of the islanders with their houses, school and church.

 

Includes: 

  • Shared Navigation 60 "with Audio Guide

     

  • Boat with coffee and Patiserie on board

  • Bilingual tour guide
  • Private Transfer in / back to the city of Tigre

 

Suggest wearing comfortable casual clothing.

html.primary:
Know Before You Book

1. Customer under 18 years old MUST be accompanied by at least one adult. A guardian traveling with children must provide proof of custody or notarized parental authorization for travel. An adult accompanying a child who is neither a parent nor guard- ian of the child should have written permission from a parent or guardian to accompany the child across the border, including the name and contact information of the parent or guardian. In consideration of the guest safety, pregnant women are only allowed to join the tour if pregnant for 24 weeks or less by the end of the trip.

2. Please be sure to provide accurate and complete information upon booking.   • Enjoy a tango performance dinner show and experience the best of Argentina - food, music, and dancing.
  • Get away from the hustle and bustle of the city for a cruise tour of the idyllic Tigre River Delta. Relax yourself when shuttling around the canals and islands.

01/05/2025-12/17/2025: Wednesday, Sunday
01/04/2026-12/16/2026: Wednesday, Sunday
No tours are available on the following dates: 04/01/2026-04/15/2026

  • 31 days or less prior to travel date: a 100% cancellation fee will be applied to the total invoice.
  • No shows: 100% cancellation charges will be incurred.
